message.content:
Yes — that’s a good fit, with one practical caveat.

The workflow can label each open response using your team’s coding scheme as the reference, so the output categories stay aligned with the definitions and exemplar quotes you’ve already used in published work. For auditability, we can also have it record things like:

- assigned category
- short justification
- relevant definition or exemplar from the coding scheme
- confidence / “needs human review” flag
- original response text
- coding scheme version or file name used

The caveat: in the currently listed workflow plugins, I don’t see a native PDF-reading input. So the safest path is either:

1. you share the coding scheme as extracted text / Markdown / Word-exported text / CSV, if possible; or  
2. an operator converts the PDF to text first, then we use that extracted text as the reference document; or  
3. if your team already has the document indexed in a retrieval store, we can use that as the reference source.

Once the coding scheme text is available, the workflow would look roughly like:

> open responses file → retrieve/consult relevant coding-scheme definitions → model assigns analytical category → save labelled responses + rationale + review flags

I’d suggest we design it so the model is not allowed to invent new categories unless you explicitly want an “other / emerging theme” pathway. Otherwise, anything that doesn’t clearly match the published scheme should be flagged for human review.

Please share the file when ready. If you can also share the next-wave response file, or tell me its format and the column containing the open responses, I can set up the labelling workflow around both.
